I'm working in a bash-based CLI tooling repo at /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster. I need a thorough map of the existing "image family" maintenance verbs and the host-side GAR (Google Artifact Registry) REST machinery, so I can implement three new path-polymorphic verbs (list / wrest / jettison) that operate on raw GAR paths.

Search breadth: very thorough. Report file paths and line numbers (file_path:line) for everything; quote short signatures, not whole files.

Find and report on ALL of the following:

1. **The image-family colophons / tabtargets.** In `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/tt/`, find every tabtarget matching `rbw-i*` (e.g. rbw-irh, rbw-irr, rbw-iah, rbw-iar, rbw-iwh, rbw-iwr, rbw-iwe, rbw-iJh, rbw-iJr, rbw-iJe). For each: the exact filename, the colophon, and what command it dispatches (the BURD_ENTRY / command line inside).

2. **The CLI partners and workbench routing.** Find where these colophons are enrolled/routed — the workbench file (likely `Tools/rbk/.../rbw_workbench.sh` or a zipper) and the CLI dispatch. Show how a colophon like `rbw-iwe` maps to a function.

3. **The wrest / jettison / inventory / audit / rekon implementation bodies.** Locate these files and their key public functions:
   - `Tools/rbk/rbflw_Wrest.sh` (image wrest from registry)
   - `Tools/rbk/rbfln_Inventory.sh` (ledger listing/enumeration)
   - any file implementing image jettison (delete a tag/version from GAR)
   - any file implementing audit / rekon (RBSIA image_audit, RBSIR image_rekon)
   For each, list the public function names and a one-line description of what each does.

4. **The host-side GAR REST primitives.** Examine `Tools/rbk/rbge_Rest.sh` (Google REST — LRO polling + API-enable), `Tools/rbk/rbfcg_GarRest.sh` (Foundry Core GAR-REST), and `Tools/rbk/rbuh_Http.sh` (Utility HTTP — JSON REST, polling). Report: the function for issuing a GAR REST GET/DELETE, any LRO-handling function (look for `rbge_lro_ok`), any "poll until gone" helper (look for `rbuh_poll_until_gone`), and how a GAR list (packages.list / tags.list / versions.list) is performed if one exists. Quote signatures.

5. **The enshrinement verbs to retire (iwe / iJe).** Pin down exactly which files implement `DirectorWrestsEnshrinedImage` (iwe) and `DirectorJettisonsEnshrinement` (iJe) — the tabtargets, the CLI handlers, the function bodies, and the `rbi_es` namespace handling. I need the complete retirement surface.

6. **The RBSI* specs.** In `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/`, list the relevant spec files: RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc, RBSIW-image_wrest.adoc, RBSIA-image_audit.adoc, RBSIR-image_rekon.adoc. For each, give a 1-2 sentence summary of what the spec covers.

7. **GAR path / layout helpers.** Find `Tools/rbk/rbgl_GarLayout.sh` and `Tools/rbk/rbga_ArtifactRegistry.sh` — report functions that construct GAR resource paths (projects/.../locations/.../repositories/.../packages/...), and any constant for the GAR repo name / location. How does existing code distinguish a `:tag` ref from an `@sha256:` ref from a bare path?

Return a structured report organized by the 7 sections above. I need enough to know exactly which files to create, which to edit, and which host-side REST primitives already exist for me to reuse.

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# z-launcher.sh — universal tabtarget trampoline.
3	#
4	# Every tt/*.sh dispatches through here:
5	#   export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.<id>_workbench.sh
6	#   exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
7	#
8	# Two responsibilities:
9	#   1. Normalize cwd to repo root so every workbench starts from a
10	#      deterministic directory regardless of where the user invoked the
11	#      tabtarget.
12	#   2. Resolve the moorings launcher named by BURD_LAUNCHER and exec it,
13	#      forwarding the tabtarget basename and user args unchanged. The
14	#      downstream launcher stub / bul_launch / bud_dispatch chain sees exactly
15	#      the argument shape it saw before this trampoline existed.
16	#
17	# BURD_LAUNCHER contract: the tabtarget exports BURD_LAUNCHER as a bare launcher
18	# basename (launcher.<id>_workbench.sh) ahead of dispatch. Every launcher
19	# co-locates in the moorings launcher dir, so the basename resolves directly —
20	# the exec line carries no launcher token and is byte-identical across every
21	# tabtarget. BURD_LAUNCHER flows downstream unchanged (it is the regime
22	# variable required by burd_regime and allowlisted by bud_dispatch).
23	#
24	# No-log behavior is NOT a launcher selection: it rides the BURD_NO_LOG env
25	# var the tabtarget exports ahead of dispatch (bul_launcher skips the BURS
26	# station load under it). The former separate nolog launcher was collapsed.
27	
28	set -u
29	
30	# Resolve own directory to an absolute path before any chdir.
31	z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
32	case "${z_dir}" in
33	  /*) ;;
34	  *)  z_dir="${PWD}/${z_dir}" ;;
35	esac
36	
37	test -n "${BURD_LAUNCHER:-}" || { echo "z-launcher: BURD_LAUNCHER unset" >&2; exit 1; }
38	
39	# Project-intimate config-dir anchor. z-launcher is the SOLE file that knows
40	# where THIS project keeps its moorings/config dir (.buk, rbmm_moorings, …).
41	# The shared kit (bul_launcher, bubc) consumes BURD_CONFIG_DIR exported below
42	# rather than hardcoding a name — so one kit serves every consumer.
43	z_moorings_dir="rbmm_moorings"
44	
45	# Resolve the launcher basename directly under the co-located launcher dir.
46	z_launcher="${z_dir}/../${z_moorings_dir}/rbml_launchers/${BURD_LAUNCHER}"
47	
48	# Fail loud on a mistyped launcher rather than dispatching silently to nothing.
49	test -f "${z_launcher}" || {
50	  echo "z-launcher: no launcher '${BURD_LAUNCHER}' (looked for ${z_launcher})" >&2
51	  exit 1
52	}
53	
54	# Normalize cwd to repo root for the dispatched workbench.
55	cd -P "${z_dir}/.." || { echo "z-launcher: cannot cd to repo root" >&2; exit 1; }
56	
57	# Hand the config-dir location to the shared launcher (absolute, cd-proof).
58	export BURD_CONFIG_DIR="${PWD}/${z_moorings_dir}"
59	
60	# Forward everything: tabtarget basename + user args.
61	exec "${z_launcher}" "${@}"
62

232	######################################################################
233	# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)
234	
235	zrbz_healthcheck() {
236	  zrbz_sentinel
237	  buz_healthcheck
238	}
239	
240	######################################################################
241	# Rust const projection (colophons → RBTDGC_)
242	
243	# rbz_emit_consts() - Emit the complete generated RBTDGC_ consts file to stdout.
244	# The single producer of rbtdgc_consts.rs content, shared by the theurge build
245	# (rbz_generate_consts) and rbq's freshness gate so the two never diverge.
246	# Two sections under one banner: the colophon group (this module binds the RBK
247	# projection prefixes — const name is RBTDGC_ + the enroll varname with its
248	# RBZ_ prefix stripped) followed by the rbcc single-homed set. rbcc_emit_consts
249	# is reachable here because every caller (rbq, the build, rblm) sources rbcc
250	# alongside rbz.
251	rbz_emit_consts() {
252	  zrbz_sentinel
253	  printf '%s\n' "// Generated by the theurge build — rbz colophons + rbcc constants. Do not edit."
254	  printf '%s\n' "// Regenerate: tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh"
255	  printf '%s\n' ""
256	  buz_emit_colophon_consts "RBTDGC_" "RBZ_" || buc_die "rbz_emit_consts: colophon emit failed"
257	  printf '%s\n' ""
258	  rbcc_emit_consts || buc_die "rbz_emit_consts: rbcc emit failed"
259	}
260	
261	# rbz_generate_consts() - Write the colophon const file, only on difference.
262	# Args: target_path
263	# Emits to a temp file, diffs against the target, and copies only when changed
264	# (stable timestamps). The single producer for the generated file — rblm and
265	# the theurge build wrapper both call this; rbq's gate verifies via rbz_emit_consts.
266	rbz_generate_consts() {
267	  zrbz_sentinel
268	
269	  local -r z_target="${1:-}"
270	  test -n "${z_target}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_consts: target path required"
271	
272	  local -r z_tmp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbz_generate_consts.rs"
273	  rbz_emit_consts > "${z_tmp}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_consts: emit failed"
274	
275	  if test -f "${z_target}" && [[ "$(<"${z_tmp}")" == "$(<"${z_target}")" ]]; then
276	    buc_log_args "Colophon consts already up to date: ${z_target}"
277	  else
278	    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_consts: failed to write ${z_target}"
279	    buc_log_args "Generated: ${z_target}"
280	  fi
281	}
282	
283	# rbz_generate_context() - Write the markdown tabtarget context, only on difference.
284	# Args: tt_dir, target_path
285	# Emits via buz_emit_context (which resolves frontispieces from tt_dir filenames),
286	# diffs against the target, and copies only when changed. Companion producer to
287	# rbz_generate_consts; the theurge build calls both, rbq's gate verifies via buz_emit_context.
288	rbz_generate_context() {
289	  zrbz_sentinel
290	
291	  local -r z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
292	  local -r z_target="${2:-}"
293	  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_context: tabtarget dir required"
294	  test -n "${z_target}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_context: target path required"
295	
296	  local -r z_tmp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbz_generate_context.md"
297	  buz_emit_context "${z_tt_dir}" > "${z_tmp}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_context: emit failed"
298	
299	  if test -f "${z_target}" && [[ "$(<"${z_tmp}")" == "$(<"${z_target}")" ]]; then
300	    buc_log_args "Tabtarget context already up to date: ${z_target}"
301	  else
302	    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_context: failed to write ${z_target}"
303	    buc_log_args "Generated: ${z_target}"
304	  fi
305	}
306	
307	######################################################################
308	# Internal sentinel
309	
310	zrbz_sentinel() {
311	  test "${ZRBZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbz not kindled - call zrbz_kindle first"
312	}

20	# pull an image from the registry to the local container runtime by locator

35	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
36

38

41	    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
42	  esac
43	  local -r z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
44	  local -r z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
45	  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
46	  test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"
47	
48	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
49	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
50	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
51	  local z_token
52	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
53	    || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
54	
55	  buc_step "Logging into container registry"
56	  local -r z_full_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${z_locator}"
57	
58	  rbgo_docker_login "${z_token}" "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}"
59	
60	  buc_step "Pulling image: ${z_full_ref}"
61	  docker pull "${z_full_ref}" || buc_die "Image pull failed"
62	
63	  local z_image_id
64	  docker inspect --format='{{.Id}}' "${z_full_ref}" > "${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null \
65	    || buc_die "Failed to get image ID"
66	  z_image_id=$(<"${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}")

68	  echo ""
69	  echo "Image wrested: ${z_full_ref}"
70	  echo "Local image ID: ${z_image_id}"
71	
72	  buc_success "Image wrest complete"
73	}

20	# tally hallmark health, rekon a hallmark/reliquary subtree, and audit hallmarks
21	# and reliquaries (Retriever for tally; Director for the rest).
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Inventory (rbfl_*)
27	
28	rbfl_tally() {
29	  zrbfl_sentinel
30	
31	  buc_doc_brief "Tally hallmarks with health status (vouched / pending / incomplete)"
32	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
33	
34	  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"
35	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" \
36	    || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
37	  local z_token=""
38	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") \
39	    || buc_die "Failed to get Retriever OAuth token"
40	
41	  buc_step "Enumerating hallmarks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
42	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"
43	
44	  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
45	    buc_info "No hallmarks found under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
46	    buc_success "Tally complete — 0 hallmarks"
47	    return 0
48	  fi
49	
50	  # Load-then-iterate. A synthetic sentinel element appended to the array
51	  # lets the final hallmark flush through the same boundary branch as every
52	  # intermediate one (single flush site).
53	  local z_lines=()
54	  local z_line=""
55	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
56	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
57	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
58	  z_lines+=("__SENTINEL__ __SENTINEL__")
59	
60	  echo ""
61	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "HALLMARK" "HEALTH" "BASENAMES"
62	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "------------------------------" "-----------" "---------"
63	
64	  # State machine over <hallmark> <basename> pairs (file was sorted by the
65	  # capture helper). Vessel is no longer encoded in the GAR path —
66	  # restoration via about/vouch metadata is AAL territory.
67	  local z_prev_h="" z_prev_bns=""
68	  local z_prev_img=0 z_prev_abt=0 z_prev_vch=0
69	  local z_count=0 z_vouched_n=0 z_pending_n=0 z_incomplete_n=0
70	  local z_i="" z_h="" z_b="" z_health=""
71	
72	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
73	    z_line="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
74	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
75	
76	    z_h="${z_line%% *}"
77	    z_b="${z_line#* }"
78	    test -n "${z_h}" || continue
79	    test -n "${z_b}" || continue
80	
81	    if test "${z_h}" != "${z_prev_h}"; then
82	      if test -n "${z_prev_h}"; then
83	        if test "${z_prev_img}" = "1" \
84	          && test "${z_prev_abt}" = "1" \
85	          && test "${z_prev_vch}" = "1"; then
86	          z_health="vouched"
87	          z_vouched_n=$(( z_vouched_n + 1 ))
88	        elif test "${z_prev_img}" = "1" \
89	          && test "${z_prev_abt}" = "1"; then
90	          z_health="pending"
91	          z_pending_n=$(( z_pending_n + 1 ))
92	        else
93	          z_health="incomplete"
94	          z_incomplete_n=$(( z_incomplete_n + 1 ))
95	        fi
96	        printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "${z_prev_h}" "${z_health}" "${z_prev_bns}"
97	        z_count=$(( z_count + 1 ))
98	      fi
99	
100	      case "${z_h}" in
101	        __SENTINEL__) break ;;
102	      esac
103	
104	      z_prev_h="${z_h}"
105	      z_prev_bns=""
106	      z_prev_img=0
107	      z_prev_abt=0
108	      z_prev_vch=0
109	    fi
110	
111	    z_prev_bns="${z_prev_bns}${z_prev_bns:+ }${z_b}"
112	    case "${z_b}" in
113	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}") z_prev_img=1 ;;
114	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}") z_prev_abt=1 ;;
115	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}") z_prev_vch=1 ;;
116	    esac
117	  done
118	
119	  echo ""
120	  buc_info "Total hallmarks: ${z_count}  (vouched: ${z_vouched_n}, pending: ${z_pending_n}, incomplete: ${z_incomplete_n})"
121	
122	  case "${z_pending_n}" in
123	    0) ;;
124	    *) buc_info "To vouch pending hallmarks:"
125	       buc_tabtarget "rbw-fV"
126	       ;;
127	  esac
128	
129	  case "${z_incomplete_n}" in
130	    0) ;;
131	    *) buc_info "To abjure incomplete hallmarks:"
132	       buc_tabtarget "rbw-fA"
133	       ;;
134	  esac
135	
136	  buc_success "Tally complete"
137	}

198	    fi
199	    printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "${z_canon}" "${z_mark}" "${z_path}"
200	  done

22	# wrest). The readonly ZRBFL_* constants the kindle sets are read globally.
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion detection
27	test -z "${ZRBFL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
28	ZRBFL_SOURCED=1
29	
30	# Source shared Foundry Core entry and the guard-free body clusters
31	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh"
32	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfli_Inscribe.sh"
33	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfly_Yoke.sh"
34	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfld_Delete.sh"
35	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfln_Inventory.sh"
36	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbflw_Wrest.sh"
37	
38	# Cross-source from the rbld (Lode) family: abjure dispatches its GAR package
39	# delete cloud-side through the build-assembly spine and the shared cloud-delete
40	# body, exactly as banish does (the cinch-blessed narrow cross into made-side
41	# delete). Both carry their own inclusion guards, so this single-source-per-
42	# process reach raises no double-source; the spine reads only rbfc-level state
43	# (zrbfc_sentinel), which this process kindles.
44	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rblds_Spine.sh"
45	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldd_Delete.sh"
46	
47	######################################################################
48	# Internal Functions (zrbfl_*)
49	
50	zrbfl_kindle() {
51	  test -z "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl already kindled"
52	
53	  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
54	  zrbfc_sentinel
55	
56	  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
57	  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
58	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
59	
60	  buc_log_args 'RBGJI inscribe step scripts (same Tools directory)'
61	  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
62	  readonly ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgji"
63	  test -d "${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJI steps directory not found: ${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}"
64	
65	  buc_log_args 'Define delete operation file prefix'
66	  readonly ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_delete_"
67	
68	  buc_log_args 'Define reliquary inscribe operation file prefix'
69	  readonly ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_reliquary_"
70	
71	  readonly ZRBFL_KINDLED=1
72	}
73	
74	zrbfl_sentinel() {
75	  zrbfc_sentinel
76	  test "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfl not kindled - call zrbfl_kindle first"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - delete cluster (guard-free, sourced by rbflk_):
20	# jettison a single image tag by locator, or abjure a whole hallmark subtree
21	# (Director credentials).
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Delete (rbfl_*)
27	
28	rbfl_jettison() {
29	  zrbfl_sentinel
30	
31	  local z_locator="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
32	
33	  # Documentation block
34	  buc_doc_brief "Jettison an image tag from the registry by locator"
35	  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. rbi_hm/H/image:H)"
36	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
37	
38	  # Validate locator parameter
39	  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"
40	
41	  # Parse locator into package path and tag
42	  case "${z_locator}" in
43	    *:*) : ;;
44	    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
45	  esac
46	  local z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
47	  local z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
48	  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
49	  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"
50	
51	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
52	
53	  # Get OAuth token using Director credentials
54	  local z_token
55	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
56	
57	  buc_require "Will jettison: ${z_locator}" "yes"
58	
59	  buc_step "Jettisoning: ${z_locator}"
60	
61	  # Jettison by tag reference
62	  local z_status_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}status.txt"
63	  local z_response_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}response.json"
64	  local z_stderr_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}stderr.txt"
65	
66	  rbuh_request "DELETE"                                                  \
67	                    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
68	                    "${z_token}"                                              \
69	                    "${z_response_file}" "${z_status_file}" "${z_stderr_file}" \
70	    || buc_die "DELETE request failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"
71	
72	  local z_http_code
73	  z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
74	  test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty"
75	
76	  if test "${z_http_code}" != "202" && test "${z_http_code}" != "204"; then
77	    local z_body="empty"
78	    if test -f "${z_response_file}"; then z_body=$(<"${z_response_file}"); fi
79	    buc_warn "Response body: ${z_body}"
80	    buc_die "Jettison failed with HTTP ${z_http_code}"
81	  fi
82	
83	  buc_success "Jettisoned or nonexistent: ${z_locator}"
84	}
85	
86	rbfl_abjure() {
87	  zrbfl_sentinel
88	
89	  local z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
90	
91	  # Documentation block
92	  buc_doc_brief "Abjure a hallmark — delete all GAR packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/"
93	  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
94	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
95	
96	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"
97	
98	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
99	  local z_token
100	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
101	
102	  # Enumerate packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/ via GAR REST API.
103	  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,
104	  # about, attest, diags). Iterating discovered children rather than a
105	  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.
106	  local -r z_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
107	  buc_step "Enumerating packages under ${z_subtree}"
108	
109	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
110	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_abjure_list"
111	
112	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
113	  rbuh_require_ok "List packages for abjure" "${z_list_infix}"
114	
115	  # GAR returns package names URL-encoded in the resource name (slashes as
116	  # %2F); decode and prefix-match to the hallmark subtree.
117	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
118	  local -r z_pkg_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}packages.txt"
119	
120	  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_subtree}" '
121	    .packages[]?.name
122	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
123	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
124	    | select(startswith($subtree))
125	  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_pkg_file}" \
126	    || buc_die "Failed to extract package names for hallmark subtree"
127	
128	  if ! test -s "${z_pkg_file}"; then
129	    buc_die "No packages found under ${z_subtree} — hallmark not present in registry"
130	  fi
131	
132	  local z_count=0
133	  local z_count_line=""
134	  while IFS= read -r z_count_line || test -n "${z_count_line}"; do
135	    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
136	  done < "${z_pkg_file}"
137	
138	  local z_confirm_msg="Will abjure ${z_count} packages under ${z_subtree}:"
139	  local z_pkg_path=""
140	  while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
141	    z_confirm_msg="${z_confirm_msg}\n  - ${z_pkg_path}"
142	  done < "${z_pkg_file}"
143	  buc_require "${z_confirm_msg}" "yes"
144	
145	  # Cloud-dispatched delete: load the enumerated package list and hand the whole
146	  # set to a single Director-run build (one build per abjure, never per package).
147	  # The in-pool step loops the list, deleting each package by convergence (GAR's
148	  # parent-before-child FAILED_PRECONDITION means a single packages.delete of an
149	  # index web removes nothing) and verifying absence via package GET to 404 — so
150	  # the build's success IS the delete outcome, closing the host trust-200 LRO
151	  # gap; see RBSCB and rbgjl06. Load-then-pass (BCG): the file is consumed
152	  # before dispatch, no FD held across the build.
153	  local z_packages=()
154	  local z_pkg_line=""
155	  while IFS= read -r z_pkg_line || test -n "${z_pkg_line}"; do
156	    test -n "${z_pkg_line}" || continue
157	    z_packages+=("${z_pkg_line}")
158	  done < "${z_pkg_file}"
159	  test "${#z_packages[@]}" -gt 0 || buc_die "No packages loaded for abjure of ${z_subtree}"
160	
161	  buc_step "Dispatching cloud delete for ${z_count} package(s) under ${z_subtree}"
162	  zrbld_cloud_delete_dispatch "${z_token}" "Abjure" "${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}" "${z_packages[@]}"
163	
164	  echo ""
165	  buc_success "Hallmark abjured: ${z_hallmark} (${z_count} packages)"
166	}
167	
168	# eof

19	# Recipe Bottle Utility HTTP - JSON REST, polling, and shared temp-file machinery

21	set -euo pipefail

24	test -z "${ZRBUH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbuh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBUH_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbuh_*)
29	
30	zrbuh_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBUH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbuh already kindled"
32	
33	  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
34	
35	  # Ensure dependencies kindled first
36	  zrbgc_sentinel
37	  zrbgo_sentinel
38	
39	  # Module prefix for temp files (rbuh owns the shared HTTP temp-file machinery)
40	  readonly ZRBUH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbuh_"
41	
42	  # Infix postfixes for HTTP operations
43	  readonly ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON="_u_resp.json"
44	  readonly ZRBUH_POSTFIX_CODE="_u_code.txt"
45	
46	  # Validate eventual consistency settings from rbgc
47	  test -n "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC:-}" || buc_die "RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC unset"
48	  test -n "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC:-}"      || buc_die "RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC unset"
49	  test -n "${RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK:-}"      || buc_die "RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK unset"
50	
51	  readonly ZRBUH_KINDLED=1
52	}
53	
54	zrbuh_sentinel() {
55	  test "${ZRBUH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbuh not kindled - call zrbuh_kindle first"
56	}
57	
58	######################################################################
59	# Predicate Functions
60	
61	rbuh_json_valid_predicate() {
62	  zrbuh_sentinel
63	  local -r z_infix="${1:-}"
64	  test -n "${z_infix}" || return 1
65	
66	  local -r z_json_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
67	  test -f "${z_json_file}" || return 1
68	
69	  jq -e . "${z_json_file}" >/dev/null 2>&1
70	}
71	
72	# Boolean: true (0) when the captured HTTP code for an infix is a 2xx success
73	# (200/201/204 — the same success set rbuh_require_ok accepts). Unlike
74	# rbuh_require_ok it does not buc_die on failure; callers branch on the result.
75	rbuh_code_ok_predicate() {
76	  zrbuh_sentinel
77	  local -r z_infix="${1:-}"
78	  test -n "${z_infix}" || return 1
79	
80	  local z_code
81	  z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_infix}") || return 1
82	  case "${z_code}" in
83	    200|201|204) return 0 ;;
84	    *)           return 1 ;;
85	  esac
86	}
87	
88	######################################################################
89	# Capture Functions
90	
91	rbuh_urlencode_capture() {
92	  zrbuh_sentinel
93	  local z_s="${1:-}"
94	  local z_out=""
95	  local z_i=0
96	  local z_c
97	  local z_hex
98	
99	  buc_log_args "Percent encoding -> ${z_s}"
100	
101	  while test ${z_i} -lt ${#z_s}; do
102	    z_c="${z_s:z_i:1}"
103	    case "${z_c}" in
104	      [A-Za-z0-9._~-]) z_out="${z_out}${z_c}" ;;
105	      *) printf -v z_hex '%%%02X' "'${z_c}"; z_out="${z_out}${z_hex}" ;;
106	    esac
107	    z_i=$((z_i + 1))
108	  done
109	
110	  buc_log_args "Encoded ${z_out}"
111	  test -n "${z_out}" || return 1
112	  echo "${z_out}"
113	}
114	
115	rbuh_json_field_capture() {
116	  zrbuh_sentinel
117	  local -r z_infix="${1}"
118	  local -r z_jq="${2}"
119	  local -r z_json_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
120	  local z_result
121	  z_result=$(jq -r "${z_jq}" "${z_json_file}")          || return 1
122	  if test -z "${z_result}" || test "${z_result}" = "null"; then return 1; fi
123	  echo "${z_result}"
124	}
125	
126	rbuh_code_capture() {
127	  zrbuh_sentinel
128	  local -r z_infix="${1}"
129	  local -r z_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_CODE}"
130	  local z_code
131	  z_code=$(<"${z_file}") || return 1
132	  test -n "${z_code}"    || return 1
133	  echo "${z_code}"
134	}
135	
136	# Apply jq filter to file, writing result to same or different file
137	rbuh_jq_file_to_file_ok() {
138	  zrbuh_sentinel
139	  local -r z_source_infix="${1:-}"
140	  local -r z_target_infix="${2:-}"
141	  local -r z_jq_filter="${3:-}"
142	
143	  test -n "${z_source_infix}" || return 1
144	  test -n "${z_target_infix}" || return 1
145	  test -n "${z_jq_filter}"    || return 1
146	
147	  local -r z_source_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_source_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
148	  local -r z_target_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_target_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
149	
150	  test -f "${z_source_file}" || return 1
151	
152	  jq "${z_jq_filter}" "${z_source_file}" > "${z_target_file}" || return 1
153	  test -f "${z_target_file}" || return 1
154	}
155	
156	######################################################################
157	# External Functions (rbuh_*)
158	
159	# JSON REST helper (hardcoded headers)
160	# Args: method url token infix [body_file]
161	# Pass "-" as body_file to read body from stdin (curl @- convention).
162	# Use this for request bodies containing secrets to avoid disk persistence.
163	rbuh_json() {
164	  zrbuh_sentinel
165	
166	  local -r z_method="${1}"
167	  local -r z_url="${2}"
168	  local -r z_token="${3}"
169	  local -r z_infix="${4}"
170	  local -r z_body_file="${5:-}"
171	
172	  local z_curl_status=0
173	  local z_attempt=0
174	
175	  while :; do
176	    z_curl_status=0
177	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
178	
179	    # Each attempt writes to uniquely-suffixed files; callers use z_infix_u
180	    z_infix_u="${z_infix}_${z_attempt}"
181	    local z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix_u}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
182	    local z_code_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix_u}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_CODE}"
183	    local z_code_errs="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix_u}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_CODE}.stderr"
184	
185	    if test -n "${z_body_file}"; then
186	      curl                                              \
187	          -sS                                           \
188	          --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
189	          --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"        \
190	          -X "${z_method}"                              \
191	          -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"         \
192	          -H "Content-Type: application/json"           \
193	          -H "Accept: application/json"                 \
194	          -d @"${z_body_file}"                          \
195	          -o "${z_resp_file}"                           \
196	          -w "%{http_code}"                             \
197	          "${z_url}" > "${z_code_file}"                 \
198	                    2> "${z_code_errs}"                 \
199	        || z_curl_status=$?
200	    else
201	      curl                                              \
202	          -sS                                           \
203	          --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
204	          --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"        \
205	          -X "${z_method}"                              \
206	          -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"         \
207	          -H "Content-Type: application/json"           \
208	          -H "Accept: application/json"                 \
209	          -o "${z_resp_file}"                           \
210	          -w "%{http_code}"                             \
211	          "${z_url}" > "${z_code_file}"                 \
212	                    2> "${z_code_errs}"                 \
213	        || z_curl_status=$?
214	    fi
215	
216	    buc_log_args 'Curl status' "${z_curl_status}"
217	    buc_log_pipe < "${z_code_errs}"
218	
219	    # Success — break out of retry loop
220	    test "${z_curl_status}" -ne 0 || break
221	
222	    rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate "${z_curl_status}" \
223	      || buc_die "HTTP request failed (curl exit ${z_curl_status})"
224	
225	    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" \
226	      || buc_die "HTTP request failed after ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts (curl exit ${z_curl_status})"
227	
228	    buc_log_args "Transient curl error (exit ${z_curl_status}), retry ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
229	    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
230	  done
231	
232	  # Register successful attempt under bare infix for capture functions
233	  cp "${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix_u}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}" "${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
234	  cp "${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix_u}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_CODE}" "${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_CODE}"
235	
236	  local z_code
237	  z_code=$(<"${z_code_file}") || buc_die "Failed to read code file"
238	  test -n "${z_code}"         || buc_die "Empty HTTP code from curl"
239	
240	  buc_log_args "HTTP ${z_method} ${z_url} returned code ${z_code}"
241	}
242	
243	# Single HTTP request — perform curl with stderr capture, return rc.
244	# Args: method url token resp_file code_file stderr_file [body_file]
245	# Returns: 0 on success, curl exit rc on failure (no buc_die — caller decides policy).
246	# Hardcoded headers: Authorization: Bearer ${token}, Accept: application/json.
247	# When body_file is non-empty: adds Content-Type: application/json, sends @body_file.
248	# Pass "-" as body_file to read body from stdin (curl @- convention).
249	# This is the BCG-conformant primitive — caller supplies temp file paths so per-attempt
250	# numbering (BCG §"In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer") is the caller's choice.
251	rbuh_request() {
252	  zrbuh_sentinel
253	
254	  local -r z_method="${1}"
255	  local -r z_url="${2}"
256	  local -r z_token="${3}"
257	  local -r z_resp_file="${4}"
258	  local -r z_code_file="${5}"
259	  local -r z_stderr_file="${6}"
260	  local -r z_body_file="${7:-}"
261	
262	  local z_curl_status=0
263	  if test -n "${z_body_file}"; then
264	    curl                                                     \
265	        -sS                                                  \
266	        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
267	        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"               \
268	        -X "${z_method}"                                     \
269	        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"                \
270	        -H "Content-Type: application/json"                  \
271	        -H "Accept: application/json"                        \
272	        -d @"${z_body_file}"                                 \
273	        -o "${z_resp_file}"                                  \
274	        -w "%{http_code}"                                    \
275	        "${z_url}" > "${z_code_file}"                        \
276	                  2> "${z_stderr_file}"                      \
277	      || z_curl_status=$?
278	  else
279	    curl                                                     \
280	        -sS                                                  \
281	        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
282	        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"               \
283	        -X "${z_method}"                                     \
284	        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"                \
285	        -H "Accept: application/json"                        \
286	        -o "${z_resp_file}"                                  \
287	        -w "%{http_code}"                                    \
288	        "${z_url}" > "${z_code_file}"                        \
289	                  2> "${z_stderr_file}"                      \
290	      || z_curl_status=$?
291	  fi
292	
293	  return "${z_curl_status}"
294	}
295	
296	rbuh_require_ok() {
297	  zrbuh_sentinel
298	  local -r z_ctx="$1"
299	  local -r z_infix="$2"
300	  local -r z_warn_code="${3:-}"
301	  local -r z_warn_message="${4:-already exists}"
302	
303	  local z_code
304	  z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_infix}") \
305	    || buc_die "${z_ctx}: failed to read HTTP code"
306	
307	  case "${z_code}" in
308	    200|201|204) return 0 ;;
309	  esac
310	
311	  if test -n "${z_warn_code}" && test "${z_code}" = "${z_warn_code}"; then
312	    buc_warn "${z_ctx}: ${z_warn_message}"
313	    return 0
314	  fi
315	
316	  local z_err=""
317	  local -r z_response_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
318	
319	  if jq -e . "${z_response_file}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
320	    z_err=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.error.message') || z_err="Unknown error"
321	  else
322	    local z_content=$(<"${z_response_file}")
323	    test -n "${z_content}" || z_content=""
324	    z_err="${z_content:0:200}"
325	    z_err="${z_err//$'\n'/ }"
326	    z_err="${z_err//$'\r'/ }"
327	    test -n "${z_err}" || z_err="Non-JSON error body"
328	  fi
329	
330	  buc_die "${z_ctx} (HTTP ${z_code}): ${z_err}"
331	}
332	
333	# Poll endpoint until HTTP 200 (for IAM/resource propagation waits)
334	rbuh_poll_until_ok() {
335	  zrbuh_sentinel
336	
337	  local -r z_label="${1}"
338	  local -r z_url="${2}"
339	  local -r z_token="${3}"
340	  local -r z_infix="${4}"
341	
342	  local z_elapsed=0
343	  while :; do
344	    local z_poll_infix="${z_infix}-${z_elapsed}s"
345	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_poll_infix}" || true
346	
347	    local z_code
348	    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_poll_infix}") || z_code=""
349	
350	    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
351	      buc_log_args "${z_label} ready after ${z_elapsed} seconds"
352	      return 0
353	    fi
354	
355	    test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" && buc_die "${z_label}: timeout after ${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s"
356	    buc_log_args "${z_label} not ready (HTTP ${z_code}), waiting ${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s..."
357	    sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
358	    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
359	  done
360	}
361	
362	# Inverse of rbuh_poll_until_ok: poll a GET endpoint until it returns HTTP 404
363	# RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK times in a row. Used after a DELETE to confirm the
364	# resource is durably gone (not merely delete-accepted) before a same-name
365	# recreate. GCP IAM's SA read path is multi-replica eventually-consistent: a
366	# post-DELETE GET flaps 200<->404 for seconds as replicas converge, so a single
367	# 404 is not durable proof — observed live as DELETE->200, GET->200,200,404
368	# ("gone"), then the recreate's preflight GET ->200 two seconds later, failing
369	# fail-loud on a stale read. Requiring consecutive 404s debounces that flap; any
370	# intervening 200 resets the streak. Total wait stays bounded by
371	# RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC.
372	rbuh_poll_until_gone() {
373	  zrbuh_sentinel
374	
375	  local -r z_label="${1}"
376	  local -r z_url="${2}"
377	  local -r z_token="${3}"
378	  local -r z_infix="${4}"
379	
380	  local z_elapsed=0
381	  local z_streak=0
382	  while :; do
383	    local z_poll_infix="${z_infix}-${z_elapsed}s"
384	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_poll_infix}" || true
385	
386	    local z_code
387	    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_poll_infix}") || z_code=""
388	
389	    if test "${z_code}" = "404"; then
390	      z_streak=$((z_streak + 1))
391	      if test "${z_streak}" -ge "${RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK}"; then
392	        buc_log_args "${z_label} durably gone after ${z_elapsed}s (${z_streak} consecutive 404s)"
393	        return 0
394	      fi
395	      buc_log_args "${z_label} read-gone (HTTP 404, streak ${z_streak}/${RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK}); confirming in ${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s..."
396	    else
397	      test "${z_streak}" -eq 0 \
398	        || buc_log_args "${z_label} reappeared (HTTP ${z_code}) — deletion not yet propagated, streak reset"
399	      z_streak=0
400	      buc_log_args "${z_label} still present (HTTP ${z_code}), waiting ${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s..."
401	    fi
402	
403	    test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" \
404	      && buc_die "${z_label}: not durably gone after ${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s (last HTTP ${z_code}, streak ${z_streak}/${RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK})"
405	    sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
406	    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
407	  done
408	}

19	# Recipe Bottle Google REST - LRO polling and API-enable patterns over rbuh
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBGE_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbge multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBGE_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbge_*)
29	
30	zrbge_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBGE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbge already kindled"
32	
33	  # Ensure dependency kindled first (rbuh owns the HTTP temp-file machinery rbge consumes)
34	  zrbuh_sentinel
35	
36	  readonly ZRBGE_KINDLED=1
37	}
38	
39	zrbge_sentinel() {
40	  test "${ZRBGE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbge not kindled - call zrbge_kindle first"
41	}
42	
43	######################################################################
44	# Capture Functions
45	
46	rbge_error_message_capture() {
47	  zrbge_sentinel
48	  local -r z_infix="${1:-}"
49	  test -n "${z_infix}" || return 1
50	
51	  if rbuh_json_valid_predicate "${z_infix}"; then
52	    rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.error.message' 2>/dev/null || return 1
53	  else
54	    return 1
55	  fi
56	}
57	
58	######################################################################
59	# External / RBTOE Pattern Functions
60	
61	# Predicate: Check if resource was newly created and apply propagation delay
62	rbge_newly_created_delay() {
63	  zrbge_sentinel
64	
65	  local -r z_infix="${1}"
66	  local -r z_resource="${2}"
67	  local -r z_delay="${3}"
68	
69	  local z_code
70	  z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_infix}") || return 1
71	
72	  if test "${z_code}" = "200" || test "${z_code}" = "201"; then
73	    buc_step "Resource ${z_resource} newly created, waiting ${z_delay}s for propagation"
74	    sleep "${z_delay}"
75	  fi
76	}
77	
78	# POST + strict LRO handling (no heuristics)
79	rbge_lro_ok() {
80	  zrbge_sentinel
81	
82	  local -r z_label="${1}"
83	  local -r z_token="${2}"
84	  local -r z_post_url="${3}"
85	  local -r z_infix="${4}"
86	  local -r z_body="${5}"
87	  local -r z_name_jq="${6}"
88	  local -r z_poll_root="${7}"
89	  local -r z_op_prefix="${8}"
90	  local -r z_poll_interval="${9:-${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}}"
91	  local -r z_timeout="${10:-${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}}"
92	
93	  buc_log_args '1) POST the request'
94	  rbuh_json "POST" "${z_post_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" "${z_body}"
95	  rbuh_require_ok "${z_label}" "${z_infix}"
96	
97	  local z_done=""
98	  z_done=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".done") || z_done=""
99	  test "${z_done}" = "true" && {
100	    local z_lro_error=""
101	    z_lro_error=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.error.message // empty') || z_lro_error=""
102	    if test -n "${z_lro_error}"; then
103	      local z_lro_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
104	      buc_warn "${z_label}: LRO completed with error — response saved: ${z_lro_resp_file}"
105	      buc_die "${z_label}: ${z_lro_error}"
106	    fi
107	    buc_log_args 'Immediate-done response -> success (no polling)'
108	    return 0
109	  }
110	
111	  buc_log_args '2) Extract op name (or return if not an LRO)'
112	  local z_name
113	  z_name=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" "${z_name_jq}") || z_name=""
114	  test -n "${z_name}" || {
115	    buc_log_args 'No LRO name present - treat as non-LRO success'
116	    return 0
117	  }
118	  buc_log_args '3) Build poll URL based on operation name format'
119	  local z_poll_url
120	  if [[ "${z_name}" =~ ^projects/.*/locations/.*/operations/ ]]; then
121	    buc_log_args '  Regional operation with fully-qualified name'
122	    z_poll_url="${z_poll_root}/${z_name}"
123	  elif [[ "${z_name}" =~ ^projects/.*/operations/ ]]; then
124	    buc_log_args '  Global operation with project prefix'
125	    z_poll_url="${z_poll_root}/${z_name}"
126	  elif test -n "${z_op_prefix}" && [[ ! "${z_name}" =~ ^${z_op_prefix} ]]; then
127	    buc_log_args '  Legacy format - apply prefix (not already present)'
128	    z_poll_url="${z_poll_root}/${z_op_prefix}${z_name}"
129	  else
130	    buc_log_args '  Use name as-is under versioned root'
131	    z_poll_url="${z_poll_root}/${z_name}"
132	  fi
133	  buc_log_args "Poll URL: ${z_poll_url}"
134	
135	  buc_log_args '4) Poll until done or timeout'
136	  local z_elapsed=0
137	  while :; do
138	    sleep "${z_poll_interval}"
139	    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + z_poll_interval))
140	
141	    local z_poll_infix="${z_infix}-poll-${z_elapsed}s"
142	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_poll_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_poll_infix}"
143	
144	    local z_code=""
145	    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_poll_infix}") || z_code=""
146	    test "${z_code}" = "200" || buc_die "${z_label}: poll failed (HTTP ${z_code})"
147	
148	    z_done=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_poll_infix}" ".done") || z_done=""
149	    test "${z_done}" = "true" && {
150	      local z_lro_error=""
151	      z_lro_error=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_poll_infix}" '.error.message // empty') || z_lro_error=""
152	      if test -n "${z_lro_error}"; then
153	        local z_lro_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_poll_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
154	        buc_warn "${z_label}: LRO completed with error — response saved: ${z_lro_resp_file}"
155	        buc_die "${z_label}: ${z_lro_error}"
156	      fi
157	      buc_log_args "${z_label}: operation completed after ${z_elapsed}s"
158	      return 0
159	    }
160	
161	    test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${z_timeout}" && buc_die "${z_label}: timeout after ${z_timeout}s"
162	    buc_log_args "Still running at ${z_elapsed}s..."
163	  done
164	}
165	
166	# RBTOE: API Enable Pattern
167	# Ensures a specified Google Cloud API is enabled in a project with idempotent behavior
168	rbge_api_enable() {
169	  zrbge_sentinel
170	
171	  local -r z_api_service="${1}"
172	  local -r z_project_id="${2}"
173	  local -r z_token="${3}"
174	
175	  test -n "${z_api_service}" || buc_die "rbge_api_enable: API service name required"
176	  test -n "${z_project_id}" || buc_die "rbge_api_enable: project ID required"
177	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "rbge_api_enable: access token required"
178	
179	  buc_log_args "Enabling API ${z_api_service} in project ${z_project_id}"
180	
181	  local -r z_infix="api-enable-${z_api_service}"
182	  local -r z_enable_url="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com/v1/projects/${z_project_id}/services/${z_api_service}.googleapis.com:enable"
183	
184	  # Attempt to enable the API
185	  rbuh_json "POST" "${z_enable_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" ""
186	
187	  local z_code
188	  z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_infix}") || buc_die "rbge_api_enable: failed to read HTTP code"
189	
190	  case "${z_code}" in
191	    200|201|204)
192	      buc_log_args "API enable request successful (HTTP ${z_code})"
193	      ;;
194	    400)
195	      # Check if already enabled
196	      local z_err
197	      z_err=$(rbge_error_message_capture "${z_infix}") || z_err="Unknown error"
198	      if [[ "${z_err}" =~ already.enabled ]] || [[ "${z_err}" =~ "already enabled" ]]; then
199	        buc_log_args "API ${z_api_service} already enabled"
200	        return 0
201	      else
202	        buc_die "rbge_api_enable (HTTP ${z_code}): ${z_err}"
203	      fi
204	      ;;
205	    *)
206	      local z_err
207	      z_err=$(rbge_error_message_capture "${z_infix}") || z_err="Unknown error"
208	      buc_die "rbge_api_enable (HTTP ${z_code}): ${z_err}"
209	      ;;
210	  esac
211	
212	  # Check if this returned an LRO that needs polling
213	  local z_operation_name
214	  z_operation_name=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".name") || z_operation_name=""
215	
216	  if test -n "${z_operation_name}"; then
217	    buc_log_args "API enable returned LRO, polling for completion"
218	    # Use the LRO polling mechanism
219	    local -r z_poll_root="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com/v1"
220	    rbge_lro_ok \
221	      "API Enable ${z_api_service}" \
222	      "${z_token}" \
223	      "${z_enable_url}" \
224	      "${z_infix}" \
225	      "" \
226	      ".name" \
227	      "${z_poll_root}" \
228	      ""
229	  fi
230	
231	  # Verify API is enabled
232	  local -r z_verify_infix="api-verify-${z_api_service}"
233	  local -r z_verify_url="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com/v1/projects/${z_project_id}/services/${z_api_service}.googleapis.com"
234	
235	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_verify_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_verify_infix}" ""
236	  rbuh_require_ok "API Enable Verify ${z_api_service}" "${z_verify_infix}"
237	
238	  local z_state
239	  z_state=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_verify_infix}" ".state") || buc_die "Failed to read API state"
240	
241	  if test "${z_state}" != "ENABLED"; then
242	    buc_die "API ${z_api_service} not enabled after request (state: ${z_state})"
243	  fi
244	
245	  buc_log_args "API ${z_api_service} confirmed enabled"
246	}

2	#

17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Core - GAR-REST cluster (guard-free, sourced by rbfck_):
20	# enumerate GAR packages and anchors, extract FROM-scratch artifacts, and resolve
21	# a hallmark's vessel from its vouch ark - all via the Registry API, no docker.
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# GAR REST (zrbfc_* / rbfc_*)
27	
28	# Internal: enumerate every <element>/<basename> pair under <subtree-root>/ via GAR REST.
29	# Writes "<element> <basename>" pairs (one per line, sorted) to ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE.
30	# An empty file is a valid result (no elements).
31	# Args: token, subtree_root (e.g., RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT, RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT)
32	# Pagination: pageSize=1000 — deferred until evidence of >1000 packages.
33	zrbfc_list_packages_capture() {
34	  zrbfc_sentinel
35	
36	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
37	  local -r z_subtree_root="${2:?Subtree root required}"
38	
39	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
40	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfc_list_packages"
41	  local -r z_subtree="${z_subtree_root}/"
42	
43	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
44	  rbuh_require_ok "List GAR packages" "${z_list_infix}"
45	
46	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
47	  local -r z_raw_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_package_list_raw.txt"
48	
49	  # GAR returns package names URL-encoded (slashes as %2F). Decode, strip the
50	  # API path prefix, filter to <subtree>/<element>/<basename> shape
51	  # (length == 2 after split excludes anything deeper or shallower), emit as
52	  # space-separated pair lines.
53	  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_subtree}" '
54	    .packages[]?.name
55	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
56	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
57	    | select(startswith($subtree))
58	    | ltrimstr($subtree)
59	    | split("/")
60	    | select(length == 2)
61	    | "\(.[0]) \(.[1])"
62	  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_raw_file}" \
63	    || buc_die "Failed to extract GAR package list"
64	
65	  local -r z_sorted_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_package_list_sorted.txt"
66	  sort "${z_raw_file}" > "${z_sorted_file}" \
67	    || buc_die "Failed to sort GAR package list"
68	
69	  # Per-package tags.list filter: skip packages with zero live tags. Post-jettison
70	  # walking-dead packages persist in GAR's package container until the depot cleanup
71	  # policy reaps the orphan children on its daily run; filtering at this read site
72	  # decouples display state from GAR's lazy reclamation cadence. See RBSCL / RBSIR.
73	  : > "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
74	  local z_element z_basename z_pkg_name z_pkg_encoded z_tag_infix z_tag_count
75	  local -i z_tag_idx=0
76	  while IFS=' ' read -r z_element z_basename; do
77	    test -n "${z_element}" || continue
78	    z_pkg_name="${z_subtree}${z_element}/${z_basename}"
79	    z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg_name//\//%2F}"
80	    z_tag_infix=$(printf 'rbfc_tags_%04d' "${z_tag_idx}")
81	    z_tag_idx=$((z_tag_idx + 1))
82	    local z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1"
83	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tag_infix}"
84	    rbuh_require_ok "List tags for ${z_pkg_name}" "${z_tag_infix}"
85	    z_tag_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_tag_infix}" '(.tags // []) | length') \
86	      || buc_die "Failed to count tags for ${z_pkg_name}"
87	    test "${z_tag_count}" -gt 0 || continue
88	    echo "${z_element} ${z_basename}" >> "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
89	  done < "${z_sorted_file}"
90	}
91	
92	# Internal: enumerate every <anchor> directly under <subtree-root>/ via GAR REST.
93	# For 1-deep categories (e.g., enshrinements) where the package itself is
94	# named <anchor> with no further basename. Writes "<anchor>" lines (one per
95	# line, sorted) to ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE. An empty file is a valid result.
96	# Args: token, subtree_root (e.g., RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT)
97	# Pagination: pageSize=1000 — deferred until evidence of >1000 packages.
98	zrbfc_list_anchors_capture() {
99	  zrbfc_sentinel
100	
101	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
102	  local -r z_subtree_root="${2:?Subtree root required}"
103	
104	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
105	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfc_list_anchors"
106	  local -r z_subtree="${z_subtree_root}/"
107	
108	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
109	  rbuh_require_ok "List GAR anchor packages" "${z_list_infix}"
110	
111	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
112	  local -r z_raw_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_anchor_list_raw.txt"
113	
114	  # Filter to <subtree>/<anchor> shape (length == 1 after split excludes
115	  # anything deeper) and emit just the anchor name.
116	  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_subtree}" '
117	    .packages[]?.name
118	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
119	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
120	    | select(startswith($subtree))
121	    | ltrimstr($subtree)
122	    | split("/")
123	    | select(length == 1)
124	    | .[0]
125	  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_raw_file}" \
126	    || buc_die "Failed to extract GAR anchor package list"
127	
128	  sort "${z_raw_file}" > "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}" \
129	    || buc_die "Failed to sort GAR anchor package list"
130	}
131	
132	# Internal: extract files from a FROM-scratch artifact in GAR to a local directory.
133	# Handles multi-platform manifest lists by picking the first platform (content is
134	# identical for architecture-independent artifacts like -about and -vouch).
135	# Args: token package tag extract_dir
136	# Returns: 0 if extraction succeeded, 1 if artifact not found in registry.
137	# Infrastructure failures (curl, jq, tar) are fatal via buc_die.
138	zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact() {
139	  zrbfc_sentinel
140	
141	  local -r z_token="$1"
142	  local -r z_package="$2"
143	  local -r z_tag="$3"
144	  local -r z_extract_dir="$4"
145	
146	  local -r z_safe_pkg="${z_package//\//_}"
147	  local -r z_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/gar_${z_safe_pkg}_${z_tag}_"
148	
149	  # HEAD check — does the artifact exist?
150	  local -r z_head_status="${z_prefix}head_status.txt"
151	  local -r z_head_response="${z_prefix}head_response.txt"
152	  local -r z_head_stderr="${z_prefix}head_stderr.txt"
153	  curl --head -s \
154	    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
155	    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
156	    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
157	    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
158	    -w "%{http_code}" \
159	    -o "${z_head_response}" \
160	    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_package}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
161	    > "${z_head_status}" 2>"${z_head_stderr}" \
162	    || buc_die "HEAD request failed for ${z_package}:${z_tag} — see ${z_head_stderr}"
163	
164	  local -r z_http_code=$(<"${z_head_status}")
165	  test "${z_http_code}" = "200" || return 1
166	
167	  # GET manifest (may be manifest list/index or single-platform manifest)
168	  local -r z_manifest="${z_prefix}manifest.json"
169	  local -r z_manifest_stderr="${z_prefix}manifest_stderr.txt"
170	  curl -sL \
171	    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
172	    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
173	    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
174	    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
175	    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_package}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
176	    > "${z_manifest}" 2>"${z_manifest_stderr}" \
177	    || buc_die "GET manifest failed for ${z_package}:${z_tag} — see ${z_manifest_stderr}"
178	
179	  # Resolve to a single-platform manifest
180	  local z_single_manifest="${z_manifest}"
181	  local -r z_media_type_file="${z_prefix}media_type.txt"
182	  jq -r '.mediaType // empty' "${z_manifest}" > "${z_media_type_file}" 2>/dev/null || true
183	  local -r z_media_type=$(<"${z_media_type_file}")
184	
185	  case "${z_media_type}" in
186	    *manifest.list*|*image.index*)
187	      # Multi-platform manifest list — pick first platform's manifest
188	      local -r z_digest_file="${z_prefix}platform_digest.txt"
189	      jq -r '.manifests[0].digest // empty' "${z_manifest}" \
190	        > "${z_digest_file}" 2>/dev/null \
191	        || buc_die "Failed to extract platform digest from manifest list"
192	      local -r z_platform_digest=$(<"${z_digest_file}")
193	      test -n "${z_platform_digest}" || buc_die "Empty platform digest in manifest list"
194	
195	      local -r z_plat_manifest="${z_prefix}plat_manifest.json"
196	      local -r z_plat_stderr="${z_prefix}plat_manifest_stderr.txt"
197	      curl -sL \
198	        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
199	        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
200	        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
201	        -H "Accept: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json" \
202	        "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_package}/manifests/${z_platform_digest}" \
203	        > "${z_plat_manifest}" 2>"${z_plat_stderr}" \
204	        || buc_die "GET platform manifest failed for ${z_package} — see ${z_plat_stderr}"
205	      z_single_manifest="${z_plat_manifest}"
206	      ;;
207	  esac
208	
209	  # Extract all layer digests. Buildkit FROM-scratch images emit one layer per
210	  # COPY instruction (rbgja04's about-image stacks six: sbom, build_info,
211	  # recipe.txt, buildkit_metadata.json, cache_before.json, cache_after.json),
212	  # so a single-layer extract loses everything past the first COPY.
213	  local -r z_layers_file="${z_prefix}layer_digests.txt"
214	  local -r z_layers_stderr="${z_prefix}layer_digests_stderr.txt"
215	  jq -r '.layers[].digest' "${z_single_manifest}" \
216	    > "${z_layers_file}" 2>"${z_layers_stderr}" \
217	    || buc_die "Failed to extract layer digests from manifest — see ${z_layers_stderr}"
218	  test -s "${z_layers_file}" \
219	    || buc_die "No layer digests in manifest for ${z_package}:${z_tag}"
220	
221	  mkdir -p "${z_extract_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create extraction directory: ${z_extract_dir}"
222	
223	  # Load-then-iterate: file fully consumed and closed before curl/tar run, so
224	  # no child process can silently consume the loop's remaining input.
225	  local z_layer_digests=()
226	  local z_layer_line=""
227	  while IFS= read -r z_layer_line || test -n "${z_layer_line}"; do
228	    z_layer_digests+=("${z_layer_line}")
229	  done < "${z_layers_file}"
230	
231	  # Fetch and extract each layer in manifest order. FROM-scratch COPY layers
232	  # add disjoint files, so accumulation needs no whiteout handling.
233	  local z_layer_digest=""
234	  local z_blob_file=""
235	  local z_blob_stderr=""
236	  local z_tar_stderr=""
237	  local z_layer_idx=0
238	  for z_layer_idx in "${!z_layer_digests[@]}"; do
239	    z_layer_digest="${z_layer_digests[$z_layer_idx]}"
240	    test -n "${z_layer_digest}" || continue
241	    z_blob_file="${z_prefix}blob_${z_layer_idx}.tar.gz"
242	    z_blob_stderr="${z_prefix}blob_${z_layer_idx}_stderr.txt"
243	    curl -sL \
244	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
245	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
246	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
247	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_package}/blobs/${z_layer_digest}" \
248	      > "${z_blob_file}" 2>"${z_blob_stderr}" \
249	      || buc_die "GET blob failed for ${z_package} layer ${z_layer_digest} — see ${z_blob_stderr}"
250	
251	    z_tar_stderr="${z_prefix}tar_${z_layer_idx}_stderr.txt"
252	    tar -xzf "${z_blob_file}" -C "${z_extract_dir}" 2>"${z_tar_stderr}" \
253	      || buc_die "Failed to extract layer ${z_layer_digest} for ${z_package}:${z_tag} — see ${z_tar_stderr}"
254	  done
255	
256	  return 0
257	}
258	
259	# Resolve a hallmark's vessel by reading the vessel field from its vouch ark.
260	# Authenticates as Retriever, fetches the vouch ark, extracts vouch_summary.json,
261	# and emits the .vessel field. Single home for hallmark→vessel lookup; callers
262	# that need vessel identity for operation prose or logging route through here.
263	# Args: hallmark
264	# Emits: vessel sigil (e.g., "rbev-busybox")
265	rbfc_vessel_for_hallmark_capture() {
266	  zrbfc_sentinel
267	
268	  local -r z_hallmark="${1:?Hallmark required}"
269	
270	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" \
271	    || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
272	  local z_token=""
273	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") \
274	    || buc_die "Failed to get Retriever OAuth token"
275	
276	  local -r z_vouch_pkg="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}"
277	  local -r z_scratch="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_for_hallmark"
278	  rm -rf "${z_scratch}" || buc_die "Failed to clear scratch dir: ${z_scratch}"
279	
280	  zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact "${z_token}" "${z_vouch_pkg}" "${z_hallmark}" "${z_scratch}" \
281	    || buc_die "Hallmark not found: ${z_hallmark} (no vouch ark at ${z_vouch_pkg}:${z_hallmark})"
282	
283	  test -f "${z_scratch}/vouch_summary.json" \
284	    || buc_die "vouch_summary.json not found in vouch ark for ${z_hallmark}"
285	
286	  local -r z_vessel_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_for_hallmark_value.txt"
287	  jq -r '.vessel // empty' "${z_scratch}/vouch_summary.json" > "${z_vessel_file}" \
288	    || buc_die "Failed to read vessel from vouch_summary.json"
289	  local -r z_vessel=$(<"${z_vessel_file}")
290	  test -n "${z_vessel}" || buc_die "Vessel field empty in vouch_summary.json"
291	
292	  printf '%s\n' "${z_vessel}"
293	}
294	
295	# eof

39	  zrbuh_sentinel

42	  readonly ZRBGA_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbga_"
43	  readonly ZRBGA_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGA_PREFIX}empty.json"

46	  # Infix values for HTTP operations
47	  readonly ZRBGA_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"

49	  readonly ZRBGA_INFIX_DELETE_REPO="delete_repo"

62	# External Functions (rbga_*)
63

172	
173	  local z_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}${RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS}/${z_location}${RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES}/${z_repo_name}"

184	                                  "${ZRBGA_INFIX_REPO_IAM_SET}" "${z_policy_file}"
185	  rbuh_require_ok "Set repository IAM policy" "${ZRBGA_INFIX_REPO_IAM_SET}"
186	
187	  buc_success "IAM policy set on repository: ${z_repo_name} in ${z_location}"
188	}
189	
190	rbga_repo_add_iam_role() {
191	  zrbga_sentinel
192	
193	  local z_repo_name="${1:-}"
194	  local z_location="${2:-${RBGC_GAR_LOCATION}}"
195	  local z_member="${3:-}"
196	  local z_role="${4:-}"
197	
198	  buc_doc_brief "Add IAM role to a member on an Artifact Registry repository"
199	  buc_doc_param "repo_name" "Name of the repository"
200	  buc_doc_param "location" "Location of the repository (optional, defaults to RBGC_GAR_LOCATION)"
201	  buc_doc_param "member" "Member to grant role to (serviceAccount:email or user:email)"
202	  buc_doc_param "role" "Role to grant (e.g., roles/artifactregistry.reader)"
203	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
204	
205	  test -n "${z_repo_name}" || buc_die "Repository name required"
206	  test -n "${z_location}" || buc_die "Location required"
207	  test -n "${z_member}" || buc_die "Member required"
208	  test -n "${z_role}" || buc_die "Role required"
209	
210	  buc_step "Adding IAM role ${z_role} to ${z_member} on repository: ${z_repo_name} in ${z_location}"
211	
212	  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
213	  local z_token
214	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
215	
216	  # Extract email from member if it's in serviceAccount:email format
217	  local z_account_email="${z_member}"
218	  if [[ "${z_member}" =~ ^serviceAccount:(.+)$ ]]; then
219	    z_account_email="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
220	  fi
221	
222	  buc_log_args 'Use rbgi_add_repo_iam_role'
223	  rbgi_add_repo_iam_role "${z_token}" "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_account_email}" "${z_location}" "${z_repo_name}" "${z_role}"
224	
225	  buc_success "Added IAM role ${z_role} to ${z_member} on repository: ${z_repo_name} in ${z_location}"
226	}
227	
228	rbga_repo_delete() {
229	  zrbga_sentinel
230	
231	  local z_repo_name="${1:-}"
232	  local z_location="${2:-${RBGC_GAR_LOCATION}}"
233	
234	  buc_doc_brief "Delete an Artifact Registry repository"
235	  buc_doc_param "repo_name" "Name of the repository to delete"
236	  buc_doc_param "location" "Location of the repository (optional, defaults to RBGC_GAR_LOCATION)"
237	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
238	
239	  test -n "${z_repo_name}" || buc_die "Repository name required"
240	  test -n "${z_location}" || buc_die "Location required"
241	
242	  buc_step "Deleting Artifact Registry repository: ${z_repo_name} in ${z_location}"
243	
244	  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
245	  local z_token
246	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
247	
248	  local z_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}${RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS}/${z_location}${RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES}/${z_repo_name}"
249	  buc_log_args "Delete Artifact Registry repo '${z_repo_name}' in ${z_location}"
250	  local z_delete_code
251	  rbuh_json "DELETE"                                                           \
252	    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}"    \
253	                            "${z_token}" "${ZRBGA_INFIX_DELETE_REPO}"
254	  z_delete_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGA_INFIX_DELETE_REPO}") || z_delete_code="000"
255	  case "${z_delete_code}" in
256	    200|204) buc_success "Repository ${z_repo_name} deleted" ;;
257	    404)     buc_info "Repository ${z_repo_name} not found (already deleted)" ;;
258	    *)
259	      local z_err
260	      z_err=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGA_INFIX_DELETE_REPO}" '.error.message') || z_err="Unknown error"
261	      buc_die "Failed to delete repository: ${z_err}"
262	      ;;
263	  esac
264	}

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_image_audit}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation produces a flat catalog of identifiers under one of the three GAR domains
5	(hallmarks, reliquaries, enshrinements)
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	via the GAR REST API implemented by
9	{gar_service}.
10	Identifiers only — no per-member presence detail (use {rbtgo_image_rekon} for that), and no health classification (use {rbtgo_hallmark_tally} for that).
11	
12	The
13	{rbtr_director}
14	audits over three domains, each backed by a distinct procedure:
15	
16	* `rbfl_audit_hallmarks` — every hallmark identifier under the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/` root
17	* `rbfl_audit_reliquaries` — every reliquary stamp under the `{gar_reliquaries_namespace}/` root
18	* `rbfl_audit_enshrinements` — every enshrined base anchor under the `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` root
19	
20	Audit vs. {rbtgo_hallmark_tally}: audit is a {rbtr_director}-tier flat-list operation across all three domains; tally is a {rbtr_retriever}-tier hallmarks-only operation that classifies each entry by basename completeness and vouch status.
21	The two roles compose without overlap.
22	
23	== Hallmarks Audit
24	
25	The
26	{rbtr_director}
27	audits hallmark identifiers:
28	
29	//axhos_step
30	. *Authenticate as
31	{rbtr_director}*:
32	.. Execute
33	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
34	.. {rbbc_store}
35	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
36	
37	//axhos_step
38	. *Enumerate Packages Under Hallmarks Root*:
39	.. {rbbc_store}
40	`«HALLMARKS_ROOT»` from `RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT` (kindled from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS`, the token `rbi_hm`)
41	.. {rbbc_store}
42	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
43	.. {rbbc_call}
44	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
45	... method: `GET`
46	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
47	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
48	.. {rbbc_require}
49	HTTP 200 response
50	.. Extract `.packages[].name`, strip the resource prefix, URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`, and retain only entries beginning with `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/`
51	.. {rbbc_store}
52	`«HALLMARK_ROWS»` as `«HALLMARK» «BASENAME»` pairs derived from those entries (a 2-deep enumeration shared with {rbtgo_image_rekon})
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Project to Unique Identifiers*:
56	.. {rbbc_store}
57	`«HALLMARKS»` as the ordered set of distinct hallmark identifiers from the first column of `«HALLMARK_ROWS»`
58	
59	//axhoot_typed_output
60	{rbtga_ark}
61	. *Display Results*:
62	.. If `«HALLMARKS»` is empty:
63	... {rbbc_show}
64	"No hallmarks found under «HALLMARKS_ROOT»/"
65	... return success with count `0`
66	.. Otherwise:
67	... {rbbc_show}
68	table header `HALLMARK`
69	... For each `«HALLMARK»` in `«HALLMARKS»`:
70	.... {rbbc_show}
71	`«HALLMARK»`
72	... {rbbc_show}
73	"Total hallmarks: «COUNT»"
74	
75	== Reliquaries Audit
76	
77	The
78	{rbtr_director}
79	audits reliquary stamps:
80	
81	//axhos_step
82	. *Authenticate as
83	{rbtr_director}*:
84	.. Execute
85	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
86	.. {rbbc_store}
87	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
88	
89	//axhos_step
90	. *Enumerate Packages Under Reliquaries Root*:
91	.. {rbbc_store}
92	`«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»` from `RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT` (kindled from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES`, the token `rbi_rq`)
93	.. {rbbc_store}
94	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base
95	.. {rbbc_call}
96	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
97	... method: `GET`
98	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
99	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
100	.. {rbbc_require}
101	HTTP 200 response
102	.. Extract `.packages[].name`, strip the resource prefix, URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`, and retain only entries beginning with `«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»/`
103	.. {rbbc_store}
104	`«RELIQUARY_ROWS»` as `«STAMP» «TOOL»` pairs derived from those entries (a 2-deep enumeration shared with {rbtgo_image_rekon})
105	
106	//axhos_step
107	. *Project to Unique Stamps*:
108	.. {rbbc_store}
109	`«STAMPS»` as the ordered set of distinct reliquary stamps from the first column of `«RELIQUARY_ROWS»`
110	
111	//axhoot_typed_output
112	{rbst_reliquary_stamp}
113	. *Display Results*:
114	.. If `«STAMPS»` is empty:
115	... {rbbc_show}
116	"No reliquaries found under «RELIQUARIES_ROOT»/"
117	... return success with count `0`
118	.. Otherwise:
119	... {rbbc_show}
120	table header `RELIQUARY-STAMP`
121	... For each `«STAMP»` in `«STAMPS»`:
122	.... {rbbc_show}
123	`«STAMP»`
124	... {rbbc_show}
125	"Total reliquaries: «COUNT»"
126	
127	== Enshrinements Audit
128	
129	The
130	{rbtr_director}
131	audits enshrined base anchors:
132	
133	//axhos_step
134	. *Authenticate as
135	{rbtr_director}*:
136	.. Execute
137	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
138	.. {rbbc_store}
139	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
140	
141	//axhos_step
142	. *Enumerate Anchors Under Enshrines Root*:
143	.. {rbbc_store}
144	`«ENSHRINES_ROOT»` from `RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT` (kindled from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES`, the token `rbi_es`)
145	.. {rbbc_store}
146	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base
147	.. {rbbc_call}
148	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
149	... method: `GET`
150	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
151	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
152	.. {rbbc_require}
153	HTTP 200 response
154	.. Extract `.packages[].name`, strip the resource prefix, URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`, and retain only entries beginning with `«ENSHRINES_ROOT»/`
155	.. {rbbc_store}
156	`«ANCHORS»` as the list of anchor segments (1-deep enumeration — enshrines are flat, one image per anchor; see helper `zrbfc_list_anchors_capture`)
157	
158	//axhoot_typed_output
159	{rbtga_ark}
160	. *Display Results*:
161	.. If `«ANCHORS»` is empty:
162	... {rbbc_show}
163	"No enshrinements found under «ENSHRINES_ROOT»/"
164	... return success with count `0`
165	.. Otherwise:
166	... {rbbc_show}
167	table header `ENSHRINE-ANCHOR`
168	... For each `«ANCHOR»` in `«ANCHORS»`:
169	.... {rbbc_show}
170	`«ANCHOR»`
171	... {rbbc_show}
172	"Total enshrinements: «COUNT»"
173	
174	//axhoc_completion
175	Each procedure completes successfully when the ordered list of identifiers — possibly empty — has been displayed and the count line emitted.
176	The empty case is non-fatal: an empty domain reports zero rather than failing, since "registry holds no entries in this domain" is a valid director observation, not an error.
177

56	.. For each retained `<hallmark>/<basename>` package, {rbbc_call}
57	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages.tags/list[GAR REST API: tags.list] for the package and {rbbc_require} at least one tag returned (HTTP 200 with non-empty `.tags[]`). Skip packages with zero tags — these are post-jettison walking-dead awaiting the depot's cleanup policy (see {rbtgo_depot_levy} step *Create Container Repository*). Filtering at this read site decouples the display from GAR's lazy reclamation cadence.
58	.. {rbbc_store}
59	`«HALLMARK_ROWS»` as `«HALLMARK» «BASENAME»` pairs derived from the live-tagged entries (a 2-deep enumeration)
60	
61	//axhos_step
62	. *Filter to Input Hallmark*:
63	.. {rbbc_store}
64	`«PRESENT»` as the set of basenames in `«HALLMARK_ROWS»` whose hallmark equals `«INPUT_HALLMARK»`
65	.. {rbbc_fatal}
66	if `«PRESENT»` is empty: "Hallmark not found: «INPUT_HALLMARK»"
67	
68	//axhoot_typed_output
69	{rbtga_ark}
70	. *Display Results*:
71	.. {rbbc_show}
72	table header `BASENAME | EXISTS | PACKAGE-PATH`
73	.. For each canonical basename in the order
74	{rbtga_ark_image},
75	{rbtga_ark_about},
76	{rbtga_ark_vouch},
77	`attest`,
78	{rbtga_ark_pouch},
79	{rbtga_ark_diags}:
80	... {rbbc_store}
81	`«MARK»` as `yes` if `«BASENAME»` ∈ `«PRESENT»`, else `no`
82	... {rbbc_store}
83	`«PATH»` as `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«INPUT_HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»` when `«MARK»` = `yes`, else the literal `(absent)`
84	... {rbbc_show}
85	row `«BASENAME» | «MARK» | «PATH»`
86	
87	== Reliquary Rekon
88	
89	The
90	{rbtr_director}
91	rekons tool images under a reliquary stamp:
92	
93	//axhopt_typed_parameter
94	{rbst_reliquary_stamp}
95	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
96	.. {rbbc_require}
97	`«INPUT_STAMP»` provided as first argument
98	.. {rbbc_fatal}
99	if missing: "Usage: rbw-irr <stamp>"
100	
101	//axhos_step
102	. *Authenticate as
103	{rbtr_director}*:
104	.. Execute
105	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
106	.. {rbbc_store}
107	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
108	
109	//axhos_step
110	. *Enumerate Packages Under Reliquaries Root*:
111	.. {rbbc_store}
112	`«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»` from `RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT` (kindled from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES`, the token `rbi_rq`)
113	.. {rbbc_store}
114	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
115	.. {rbbc_call}
116	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
117	... method: `GET`
118	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
119	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
120	.. {rbbc_require}
121	HTTP 200 response
122	.. Extract `.packages[].name`, strip the resource prefix, URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`, and retain only entries beginning with `«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»/`
123	.. For each retained `<stamp>/<tool>` package, {rbbc_call}
124	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages.tags/list[GAR REST API: tags.list] for the package and {rbbc_require} at least one tag returned (HTTP 200 with non-empty `.tags[]`). Skip packages with zero tags — these are post-jettison walking-dead awaiting the depot's cleanup policy (see {rbtgo_depot_levy} step *Create Container Repository*). Filtering at this read site decouples the display from GAR's lazy reclamation cadence.
125	.. {rbbc_store}
126	`«RELIQUARY_ROWS»` as `«STAMP» «TOOL»` pairs derived from the live-tagged entries (a 2-deep enumeration)
127	
128	//axhos_step

132	.. {rbbc_fatal}
